channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-22
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. In the scienceverse/metacheck project, Lakens addressed a critical issue with code_check crashing and added new functions to load paper databases, demonstrating the project's commitment to stability and functionality. Meanwhile, the auto-zcurve project saw a revision of its validation note and the addition of a citation, highlighting the importance of rigorous validation and academic integrity. Furthermore, the output-it-forward project now has a Chrome Webstore link, making it more accessible to users. These updates showcase the connections between our projects, with a focus on improving user experience, validation, and functionality. As our community continues to grow and collaborate, we celebrate these milestones and look forward to the innovative developments that will emerge from these efforts.

113 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

docpluck31 commits
regcheck31 commits
coarse30 commits
metacheck17 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: escicheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The first commit synced changes from `escicheckapp@bd6f007`, which corresponds to `effectcheck v0.6.5`, as stated in the commit message. The second commit, also on the `main` branch, synced changes from `escicheckapp@dec37b1`, which is associated with `effectcheck v0.6.4`. These commits brought the `main` branch up to date with versions `v0.6.4` and `v0.6.5` of `effectcheck`. The changes were made by Gilad Feldman, who referenced issues `#18` and `#19` in the commit messages.

2 commit(s) today:

  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1f1a89a [main] sync from escicheckapp@e060e98 (effectcheck v0.6.2) (#16) — Gilad Feldman
  • f7e25f6 [sync/20260525-150441-b801333] sync from escicheckapp@b801333 (effectcheck v0.6.2) — github-actions[bot]
  • d0db89d [main] sync from escicheckapp@5093197 (effectcheck v0.6.1) (#14) — Gilad Feldman
  • a44f236 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1ebe05f (effectcheck v0.5.18) (#13) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change involved adding a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned. The commit message does not specify any additional details about the link or its purpose. There are no other branches or commits to report on in this update.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
